title: Not another gospel; Paul’s call
summary_text: Paul apostle not from men—through Jesus Christ and God who raised him. Astonished they turn to different gospel—if anyone preaches other than received, let him be accursed. Paul’s gospel not from man but revelation of Jesus Christ.
key_points: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you.
But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any ‘good news’ other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed.
I didn’t receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but it was revealed to me by Jesus Christ.
evidence_phrases: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting
there isn’t another ‘good news.’
let him be cursed
I didn’t receive it from man
it was revealed to me by Jesus Christ

title: Justified by faith; Peter rebuked
summary_text: Paul went to Jerusalem privately to confirm gospel to Gentiles. Titus not compelled circumcised. Peter ate with Gentiles then drew back—Paul rebuked: Jews live like Gentiles not by works of law—why compel Gentiles Judaize? Crucified with Christ; live by faith in Son.
key_points: We know that a man is not justified by the works of the law.
I withstood him to his face, because he stood condemned.
I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I that live.
I live in the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up for me.
evidence_phrases: a man is not justified by the works of the law
I withstood him to his face
I have been crucified with Christ
I live in the faith of the Son of God
I don’t reject the grace of God

title: Law schoolmaster; sons and heirs
summary_text: Foolish Galatians—who bewitched you? Received Spirit by works of law or hearing faith? Abraham believed God—those of faith are sons. Law was schoolmaster until Christ—we sons through faith. No Jew Greek bond free male female—all one in Christ.
key_points: Who has bewitched you not to obey the truth?
Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?
Know therefore that those who are of faith are children of Abraham.
The law has been our tutor to bring us to Christ.
There is neither Jew nor Greek … for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
evidence_phrases: Who has bewitched you
Did you receive the Spirit
those who are of faith are children of Abraham
The law has been our tutor
you are all children of God, through faith
There is neither Jew nor Greek

title: Slavery and sonship; allegory Hagar Sarah
summary_text: Heir as child differs not from slave until time appointed. God sent Son born of woman under law to redeem under law—we receive adoption. No longer slaves but sons—Abba Father. Allegory: Hagar Sinai bondage; Sarah Jerusalem above is free—we children of promise.
key_points: When we were children, we were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world.
God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law.
That we might receive adoption as children.
Because you are sons, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, ‘Abba, Father!’
The Jerusalem above is free, which is the mother of us all.
evidence_phrases: we were held in bondage
God sent out his Son
that we might receive adoption
Abba, Father!
Cast out the servant and her son
we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman

title: Stand fast; walk in Spirit; fruit
summary_text: Christ freed us—stand fast, don’t be entangled yoke of bondage. Faith working through love. Bite and devour one another—walk in Spirit, fulfill not lust of flesh. Fruit of Spirit vs works of flesh—crucified flesh with passions.
key_points: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free.
For through the Spirit, by faith, a persistent hope of righteousness is in us.
Walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.
The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, gentleness, and self-control.
Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh.
evidence_phrases: Stand firm therefore in the liberty
faith working through love
Walk by the Spirit
the fruit of the Spirit is love
Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh

title: Bear burdens; sowing and reaping
summary_text: Restore gentleness; bear one another’s burdens—law of Christ. Do not be deceived—God not mocked; sow to flesh reap corruption, to Spirit reap eternal life. Boast only in cross—world crucified to me. Israel of God peace mercy.
key_points: Brothers, even if a man is caught in some fault, you who are spiritual restore such a one.
Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.
God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.
For neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.
But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.
evidence_phrases: restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness
Bear one another’s burdens
God is not mocked
a new creation
boast, except in the cross
the Israel of God
